title: Undersampling and Oversampling
concept: We can balance the classes by either dropping rows from the majority class (undersampling) or duplicating rows from the minority class (oversampling).

exercises.prompt: Recap: What is the main danger of imbalanced data?

options.text: The model can achieve high accuracy by just guessing the most common class.
The model will always output negative numbers.
The dataset will take up too much memory.

exercises.explain: Yes! Models easily cheat by always guessing the majority class.

cards.title: Tuning the scales
cards.body: If you want to train a dog to spot truffles (rare) among common mushrooms, you can't just show them 1,000 normal mushrooms and 1 truffle. They'll ignore the truffle. To teach them, you need to balance their practice!

cards.title: Balancing the Scales
cards.body: Resampling is the process of adjusting the number of examples in each class to make them equal. The two simplest ways are undersampling (making the large group smaller) and oversampling (making the small group larger).

cards.title: The Party Playlist
cards.body: Imagine you want a playlist with Pop songs (common) and Jazz songs (rare). To balance it, you can either delete a bunch of Pop songs (undersampling) or play the same few Jazz songs on repeat (oversampling).

cards.title: Balancing Customer Feedback
cards.body: A company has 10,000 positive reviews and only 100 negative reviews. To train a model to spot complaints, they can either randomly pick just 100 positive reviews (undersampling) or duplicate their 100 negative reviews 100 times (oversampling).

cards.title: Adding data always makes it better
cards.body: You might think oversampling is always best because you keep all your data. However, duplicating the exact same rare examples on repeat can make your model "overfit" — it memorizes those specific examples instead of learning general patterns.

cards.title: Undersampling vs. Oversampling
cards.body: How they differ in modifying class sizes:

left.text:
Removes examples from the majority class.
Pro: Faster training.
Con: Loses useful information.

right.text:
Duplicates examples from the minority class.
Pro: Keeps all information.
Con: Risk of memorization.

exercises.prompt: What is a major downside of undersampling the majority class?

options.text: We throw away potentially valuable information from the deleted data.
It makes the model train much slower.
It increases the size of the dataset on disk.
It forces the model to use only continuous numbers.

exercises.explain: By deleting majority examples, we lose any unique patterns or details those deleted rows contained.

exercises.prompt: True or false: Oversampling involves deleting examples from the majority class.
exercises.explain: False. Oversampling adds or duplicates examples in the minority class. Deleting from the majority is undersampling.

exercises.sentence: Duplicating rare examples to balance a dataset is called ___.
exercises.explain: Oversampling increases the minority class size, typically by duplicating existing rare records.

exercises.prompt: Which of the following is an example of undersampling?

options.text: A fraud detection dataset with 1,000,000 safe and 1,000 fraudulent sales is balanced by randomly selecting 1,000 of the safe sales and keeping all fraudulent ones.
A dataset is balanced by copying the 1,000 fraudulent sales 1,000 times.
A dataset is balanced by calculating the average value of all transaction amounts.

exercises.explain: Selecting a small subset of the majority class (safe sales) to match the minority count is undersampling.

exercises.prompt: You have a dataset of 5,000 non-spam emails and 50 spam emails. You decide to balance it by duplicating the 50 spam emails 100 times so you have 5,000 of each. What risk did you just introduce to your model?

options.text: The model might memorize the exact 50 spam emails and fail to recognize new, slightly different spam emails.
The model will lose all information about non-spam emails.
The model will run 100 times slower due to column scaling.
The model will convert the email text into numerical values incorrectly.

exercises.explain: Spot on! Simple oversampling (copying data) can cause the model to overfit (memorize) the exact rare examples instead of finding general trends. Next, let's look at how to create brand new, realistic examples!
